No Poverty.reason: The study of "International Investment" and "global monetary economics" relates to providing access to financial services, a key component of poverty reduction strategies (Target 1.4).

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops "Theoretical foundations of professional knowledge" for future employment (Target 4.4).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course on "International Investment" and "global monetary economics" is central to understanding economic growth (Target 8.1) and strengthening financial institutions and services (Target 8.10).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: "International Investment" is a primary driver for infrastructure development and industrialization (Targets 9.1, 9.a), and financial markets provide access to financial services for enterprises (Target 9.3).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course covers "global monetary economics" and financial markets, directly relating to their regulation (Target 10.5) and financial flows to developing countries (Target 10.b).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The core competence to "identify ethical, socially responsible, and sustainability issues" directly supports encouraging companies to adopt sustainable practices through investment decisions (Target 12.6).
evidence: To understand and identify ethical, socially responsible, and sustainability issues

Climate Action.reason: Inferred link. "International Investment" is a key mechanism for mobilizing finance for climate action (Target 13.a), especially given the course's focus on "sustainability issues".

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The study of global financial markets relates to developing transparent institutions (Target 16.6) and strengthening developing country participation in global governance (Target 16.8).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course is core to global financial partnerships, covering mobilizing resources (Target 17.3), investment promotion (Target 17.5), and enhancing global macroeconomic stability (Target 17.13).
